title: Lane classifier: decide a backlog row's lane safety, or refuse it
action: This is the mechanism D6 has been asserting without owning, and approach.md names it the HIGH risk that must be proved before the loop ever runs unattended. Usage: `node classify-lane.mjs <PBI-ID> [--backlog PATH]`, default backlog docs/backlog.md - the --backlog override exists so the refusal cases can be proved against fixtures without touching the real backlog. Emit JSON {pbi, lane, hard_gate_flags[], lane_safe, reason}. The field is lane_safe, NOT dispatchable: this answers only 'is this row's lane safe for an unattended agent', which is one input to D1's four-condition dispatchable set, never the whole of it. Count the mode-gate risk flags from the row text using bee-planning's own list, and treat the hard-gate flags (auth, authorization, data loss, audit/security, external provider, validation removal) as decisive. Return lane_safe false when the lane is high-risk, when any hard-gate flag matches, AND whenever the row cannot be classified with confidence: no row found, empty or unparseable text, or a signal the rules do not cover. Fail closed is the whole point; an unclassifiable row must never come back safe. The reason string must name the specific flag or the specific reason confidence failed, never a bare 'high-risk'. Directory name is herdr-orchestrating (D21). Write byte-identical copies into both skill roots.

truths: A row mentioning auth or data loss returns lane_safe false and names the flag
A PBI id with no row returns lane_safe false, not an error and not true
A row with empty or unparseable text returns lane_safe false
An ordinary low-risk row returns lane_safe true and a lane value below high-risk
--backlog PATH reads the given file instead of docs/backlog.md
The reason always names a specific cause

artifacts.substantive: fail-closed lane classifier emitting JSON, with a backlog override

prohibitions: Do not return lane_safe true for anything the rules cannot classify
Do not write to docs/backlog.md or any fixture; read only
Do not name any directory bee-*

verify:
set -e; C=.claude/skills/herdr-orchestrating/scripts/classify-lane.mjs; mkdir -p .bee/tmp; FX=.bee/tmp/apo-classify-fixture.md; trap 'rm -f "$FX"' EXIT; printf '| ID | PBI | Status | Ghi chu |
|---|---|---|---|
| PBI-900 |  | in-flight |  |
' > "$FX"; F() { node "$C" "$1" ${2:+--backlog "$2"} | node -e "let s='';process.stdin.on('data',d=>s+=d).on('end',()=>{const j=JSON.parse(s);console.log([j.lane_safe,j.lane,(j.reason||'').length].join('|'))})"; }; test "$(F PBI-044 | cut -d'|' -f1)" = false; test "$(F PBI-044 | cut -d'|' -f3)" -gt 0; test "$(F PBI-999 | cut -d'|' -f1)" = false; test "$(F PBI-900 "$FX" | cut -d'|' -f1)" = false; test "$(F PBI-038 | cut -d'|' -f1)" = true; test "$(F PBI-038 | cut -d'|' -f2)" != 'high-risk'; diff -q "$C" .agents/skills/herdr-orchestrating/scripts/classify-lane.mjs; echo CLASSIFIER_FAILS_CLOSED
